data.current_description:
Create a new webhook subscription. Webhooks allow you to receive HTTP POST notifications when events occur in your account.

**Supported Events:**
- `hours:created` - When a new time entry is created
- `hours:updated` - When a time entry is updated
- `hours:deleted` - When a time entry is deleted
- `projects:created` - When a new project is created
- `projects:updated` - When a project is updated
- `projects:deleted` - When a project is deleted
- `labels:created` - When a new label is created
- `labels:updated` - When a label is updated
- `labels:deleted` - When a label is deleted
- `forecasts:created` - When a new forecast is created
- `forecasts:updated` - When a forecast is updated
- `forecasts:deleted` - When a forecast is deleted

**Security:**
When a `secret_token` is provided, each webhook request will include an `X-Signature` header containing an HMAC-SHA256 signature of the request body. You can use this to verify the authenticity of incoming webhook requests.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new webhook subscription to receive HTTP POST notifications for account events. Specify the target URL and one or more event types to subscribe to (hours:created, hours:updated, hours:deleted, projects:created, projects:updated, projects:deleted, labels:created, labels:updated, labels:deleted, forecasts:created, forecasts:updated, forecasts:deleted). Optionally provide a secret_token to enable HMAC-SHA256 request signature verification via the X-Signature header. Returns the created webhook object with a unique ID. The webhook will immediately begin receiving notifications for subscribed events.
data.reasoning: Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about what the operation returns and the immediate side effect of the webhook becoming active. Added explicit information about return value and activation behavior to improve AI agent understanding of the operation's outcome.

data.current_description:
This is a documentation-only endpoint describing the webhook event payloads.

When you subscribe to webhook events, Timely will send HTTP POST requests to your configured URL with the following payloads:

| Event | Payload Schema |
|-------|----------------|
| `hours:created` | Hour |
| `hours:updated` | Hour |
| `hours:deleted` | Hour |
| `projects:created` | Project |
| `projects:updated` | Project |
| `projects:deleted` | Project |
| `labels:created` | Label |
| `labels:updated` | Label |
| `labels:deleted` | Label |
| `forecasts:created` | Forecast |
| `forecasts:updated` | Forecast |
| `forecasts:deleted` | Forecast |

See the webhook events below for the exact payload schemas.
data.summary_suggestion: View available webhook event types and schemas
data.description_suggestion: Documentation-only endpoint that describes all available webhook event types and their corresponding payload schemas. This endpoint does not perform any action but provides reference information for webhook subscribers. Returns a mapping of webhook events (hours:created, hours:updated, hours:deleted, projects:created, projects:updated, projects:deleted, labels:created, labels:updated, labels:deleted, forecasts:created, forecasts:updated, forecasts:deleted) to their respective schema objects (Hour, Project, Label, Forecast). Use this to understand the structure of payloads your webhook endpoint will receive when subscribed to these events.

Partial success is possible - some records may succeed while others fail with per-record error details. Time entries are locked from editing once marked as billed.
